economic_notes: Nation has investments in Six Nations Ventures L.P. (16.6%), Uujo Development L.P. (100%), and Uujo Management G.P. Ltd. (100%). Also holds shares in Cardinal Energy Ltd. and investment in EBA Development Corp. Active in oil and gas royalties, cattle operations (Doig River Cattle L.P.), timber and contracting (reorganized into Uujo Development L.P.). Community Development Trust holds heritage and agriculture benefits settlement funds.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2022, not 2024. Province is BC (Doig River First Nation is in northeastern BC), auditor office is in Edmonton AB. Consolidated entities include Doig River Cattle L.P., Doig Not For Profit Trust, and Community Development Trust. GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Six Nations Ventures L.P. (16.6%), Uujo Development L.P. (100%), Uujo Management G.P. Ltd. (100%). ISC TLE debt of $6,238,214 is separate from long-term debt on balance sheet. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($3,755,008) + First Nation Health Authority ($503,578) + Employment and Social Development Canada ($413,730) + Other government funding ($231,325) + Province of BC ($5,039,713) + CMHC ($0) = $9,943,354; Grant ($134,981) added gives $10,078,335 — used conservative sum of clearly identified government sources. Large deferred revenue increase from ISC ($6,186,767 vs $1,676,481 prior year) is notable. Income allocations from Nation Businesses ($2,758,357) recorded as other income. RBC term loan of $8,157,047 is interest-only with no principal repayment scheduled in current year; annual debt service reflects only actual principal repaid per cash flow statement.
